title: Stage 4 Phase 1: Clip bounds non-binding (5-seed paired diagnostic)

content.hypothesis: alpha_clip_lo (0.3) and alpha_clip_hi (1.8) are binding structural parameters — varying them produces measurable MI gap differences, making them viable Stage 4 adaptive targets. Clip bounds define the reachable state space for alpha, with 15% lower-bound saturation confirming the lower bound is structurally active.
content.method: 5 paired seeds, K=6, n_perm=8, n_trials=6. Conditions: CANONICAL [0.3,1.8], WIDE_LO [0.1,1.8], NARROW_LO [0.5,1.8], NARROW_TIGHT [0.5,1.6], WIDE_HI [0.3,2.8], ASYMM_LO [0.05,1.8], ASYMM_HI [0.3,2.8]. Paired seeds across conditions to avoid confounding seed variance (c024).
content.result:
FAILED. Kill criterion met — all p >= 0.096. MI gap range across all conditions = only 5.8% of canonical. No condition significantly outperforms canonical.

Key numbers:
- Canonical [0.3, 1.8]: mean MI gap = +0.6274, CV=14.8%
- ASYMM_LO [0.05, 1.8]: p=0.096, d=0.744 — closest to signal, still misses significance
- ASYMM_HI [0.3, 2.8]: p=0.802, d=0.112 — pure null
- NARROW_TIGHT [0.5, 1.6]: p=0.096 (estimated), d=0.610, mean=+0.6636 — all 5 seeds above canonical

Saturation paradox: lower clip (0.3) is hit by 15.1% of alpha updates — it IS structurally active. Yet MI is insensitive to its value. Being active is not the same as being binding.

metrics.ASYMM_LO_0.05_1.8: p=0.096, d=0.744 — closest candidate, not significant
metrics.NARROW_TIGHT_0.5_1.6: all 5 seeds above canonical, d=0.610, p=0.096 (borderline)
metrics.saturation_lower: 15.1% of alpha updates hit lower bound
metrics.saturation_upper: 0.3% of alpha updates hit upper bound

content.root_cause: Clip bounds define the reachable state space but the system's actual operating range is well within the bounds for most conditions. The lower bound's 15.1% saturation rate does not translate to binding constraint — the system can achieve equivalent MI with different bounds because alpha values near the boundary are not computationally critical for mutual information. The paradox: parameter activity (hitting the bound) ≠ parameter binding (performance depends on value).
content.implication:
Architecture review trigger has fired. All plasticity rule structural parameters are now confirmed non-binding:
- eta: vacuous (Stage 3, 7 sessions, c011)
- threshold: non-binding (Stage 4, c023)
- alpha_clip_lo/hi: non-binding (Stage 4, this entry)
- Branch weights (amplify_mult, drift_mult, symmetry_break_mult): scaling (Stage 6)
- beta/gamma: permanently blocked (c001-c007)

Two paths for team-lead ruling:
1. Amendment 1 path: run adaptive clip bounds to satisfy Criterion 2, then declare Stage 4 vacuous
2. Stage 6 path: skip topology (Stage 5) and functional form adaptation (Stage 6 = functional form becomes adaptive)

BORDERLINE FINDING: NARROW_TIGHT [0.5, 1.6] shows consistent direction (all 5 seeds above canonical, d=0.610) but p=0.096 at n=5, CV=14.8%. This could be genuine (+5.8% MI) or the same noise pattern as threshold's false positive. Team-lead must rule on whether to validate with 10-seed test or dismiss.

narrow_tight_analysis.interpretation_A: Genuine: narrower range reduces alpha noise, forcing cells to work harder within a tighter range, improving discrimination. +5.8% improvement would be meaningful.
narrow_tight_analysis.interpretation_B: Noise: threshold's ASYM_HI appeared to show +20% at n=3 (p<0.05) and was pure noise at n=10. NARROW_TIGHT shows smaller effect at n=5 — may collapse at n=10. Key difference: CV=14.8% here vs 37% for threshold, so 5-seed result is more reliable.
narrow_tight_analysis.recommendation: Flag for team-lead decision. The CV=14.8% makes this more credible than the threshold false positive, but it is still subthreshold. A targeted 10-seed test (~30 min) would resolve it definitively.

constraints_implied: alpha_clip_lo/hi are non-binding — structurally active (15% saturation) but performance-insensitive across meaningful range
Parameter activity (hitting bounds) does not imply parameter binding (performance depends on value)

addendum.note: REVISED: This entry's conclusion (clip bounds non-binding) was based on single-K K=6 diagnostics with CV≈14.8%. Multi-K reanalysis (K=[4,6,8,10], CV≈5.1%) reveals clip bounds ARE BINDING (d=7.583, p=0.000 for VERY_NARROW [0.7,1.3]). The original conclusion was a methodological false negative due to high variance. See entry 042 for multi-K validation and entry 041 for methodology discovery c027.
addendum.impact: Stage 4 prioritization inverted: clip bounds should be PRIMARY Phase 2 target (d=7.583), not secondary.
